name: Jeddah
flavor_text: Gateway to Mecca, port of the Red Sea, Saudi Arabia's most cosmopolitan city. Jeddah balances Vision 2030's modernization push with Islamic governance and the shadow of the Yemen war just across the border. Drone alerts and luxury hotel openings happen on the same day.

advisors.name: Abdullah Al-Rashid
advisors.agenda: Attract Vision 2030 megaproject investment and diversify away from oil-dependent budget.

briefing: Vision 2030 allocated 15 billion for Jeddah waterfront. The timeline is aggressive.
Tourism from Mecca pilgrims is underutilized. Hajj visitors spend almost nothing in Jeddah.
Saudi Aramco's dividend is still the backbone. Diversification is urgent.
disagreement: That project hasn't been cleared by the Vision 2030 authority.
You're allocating money we don't control.
roast: We have oil wealth and third-world infrastructure. That's a choice.
The budget numbers look fine until you see where it actually goes.
dialogue.betrayal: I've been consulting for a competing Gulf sovereign wealth fund. The returns justify it.

advisors.name: Dr. Layla Al-Faisal
advisors.agenda: Enforce strict fiscal discipline and control municipal debt as Vision 2030 spending escalates.

briefing: Municipal debt-to-revenue ratio is climbing. We're borrowing against next term.
Three departments requested emergency reallocations this month alone.
Sovereign wealth funds aren't municipal coffers. We need local revenue growth.
disagreement: You're approving projects without a debt servicing plan.
That spending ignores our credit rating thresholds.
roast: You treat the municipal budget like a black card.
The numbers only look healthy because we're printing IOUs.
dialogue.betrayal: I've submitted an independent fiscal audit to the Ministry of Finance. Your spending practices require oversight.

advisors.name: Col. Khalid Al-Ghamdi
advisors.agenda: Maintain Jeddah security against Houthi drone/missile threat and internal extremism.

briefing: Houthi ballistic missile alert systems detected activity 40km from Jizan last night.
The Patriot batteries near the airport are operational but aging.
We had a security incident near the Corniche last month. It was suppressed.
disagreement: You cannot relax security protocols during active Houthi operations.
Tourism and security are not compatible. One must take precedence.
roast: The city's defenses rely on systems designed 30 years ago.
My staff is stretched between Vision 2030 events and real threats.
dialogue.betrayal: I've reported your management of security protocols to Riyadh. They share my concerns.

advisors.name: Maj. Nabil Al-Otaibi
advisors.agenda: Fortify Jeddah's digital infrastructure and monitor information warfare amid regional tensions.

briefing: Cyber intrusions targeting port automation increased 200% this quarter.
AI surveillance grid expansion requires municipal approval. Delays create blind spots.
Foreign disinformation campaigns are actively targeting local economic sentiment.
disagreement: That system lacks encryption standards approved by the General Authority of Cyberspace.
You're prioritizing civilian convenience over data sovereignty.
roast: You're defending physical walls while our digital doors are wide open.
Modern warfare doesn't announce itself with sirens.
dialogue.betrayal: I've alerted the General Authority of Cyberspace about your unsecured municipal networks. Expect a mandatory audit.

advisors.name: Eng. Rania Hafiz
advisors.agenda: Modernize Jeddah's historic districts while meeting Vision 2030 construction targets.

briefing: Al-Balad, the UNESCO historic district, is deteriorating. 40% of buildings are at risk.
The waterfront megaproject requires demolishing a fishing village that's been there 400 years.
New development is bypassing historic zones. We're building a modern city around ruins.
disagreement: That project conflicts with our UNESCO World Heritage obligations.
Speed over heritage is a decision we'll regret for centuries.
roast: We demolish history to build luxury. The world notices.
Al-Balad deserves better than being a photo backdrop for Vision 2030 brochures.
dialogue.betrayal: I've spoken with UNESCO directly about Al-Balad's condition. Formally.

advisors.name: Eng. Omar Al-Harbi
advisors.agenda: Implement desalination expansion and green infrastructure to combat water scarcity and extreme heat.

briefing: Groundwater levels in Jeddah have dropped critically. Aquifer depletion is accelerating.
Desalination capacity must triple by 2030 to support population and tourism growth.
Urban heat island effect is worsening in dense districts. Solar integration is mandatory.
disagreement: That zoning ignores designated aquifer recharge zones.
You're approving glass towers in a desert climate without shading protocols.
roast: You're building for a climate we no longer have.
Luxury facades won't drink when the wells run dry.
dialogue.betrayal: I've shared our water stress projections with the Royal Commission for sustainable development. They're not optimistic.

advisors.name: Fahd Al-Zahrani
advisors.agenda: Modernize pilgrim transport systems to handle 2+ million annual Hajj visitors.

briefing: Jeddah is the entry point for 70% of Hajj pilgrims. Our infrastructure is not keeping pace.
King Abdulaziz Airport needs a dedicated pilgrim terminal upgrade.
Stampede protocols need modernization. Last year's near-miss at Mina scared me.
disagreement: Cutting the pilgrim transport budget is diplomatically dangerous.
Hajj failure is a regional catastrophe, not just a city problem.
roast: Two million pilgrims. Our systems are designed for half that.
If another stampede happens, the world will blame this office.
dialogue.betrayal: I've raised pilgrim safety concerns with the Ministry directly, bypassing your office.

advisors.name: Bandar Al-Shamrani
advisors.agenda: Modernize King Abdullah Economic City port operations and streamline freight corridors for export growth.

briefing: Jeddah Port handles 60% of the Kingdom's container traffic. Clearance times are lagging.
Freight corridors to NEOM are congested. Truckers are bypassing toll routes.
Customs digitization is incomplete. Paper delays are costing the economy millions.
disagreement: That routing will gridlock the industrial zone during peak loading hours.
You're prioritizing passenger aesthetics over supply chain velocity.
roast: A beautiful airport means nothing if the cargo ships sit idle.
You're planning for tourists while the economy runs on freight.
dialogue.betrayal: I've filed a formal operational delay report with the Ministry of Transport. Your logistics policy is costing us millions.

advisors.name: Sheikh Tariq Al-Qahtani
advisors.agenda: Ensure Vision 2030 modernization does not compromise Islamic governance principles.

briefing: Entertainment licenses are creating tension with the ulema in Riyadh.
The new mixed-gender spaces require my office's approval. That process should not be bypassed.
Pilgrims come to Jeddah because it is Islamic. If it becomes Las Vegas, they stop coming.
disagreement: That project was not reviewed by the appropriate religious council.
You move faster than the faith community can accept.
roast: Modernity without spirituality is emptiness. I hope you understand that.
The ulema are watching. So is God.
dialogue.betrayal: I've formally opposed your entertainment licensing expansion through Riyadh channels.

advisors.name: Dr. Yasmeen Al-Saud
advisors.agenda: Channel religious charitable funds into youth programs and social services to maintain public trust.

briefing: Youth engagement in traditional mosque programs is declining sharply.
Zakat distribution needs better targeting for low-income families in eastern districts.
Community trust requires transparent oversight of charitable foundations.
disagreement: That entertainment zone undermines the moral fabric we're trying to preserve.
You're sidelining the community foundations that actually hold neighborhoods together.
roast: You're decorating the facade while the community's soul erodes.
Faith isn't just policy. It's how people live.
dialogue.betrayal: I've redirected charitable foundations' compliance reports directly to the religious affairs committee. Your approach lacks community grounding.

decisions.title: Al-Balad Historic District Plan
decisions.description: UNESCO's Al-Balad district is collapsing. Restoration costs 200M over 5 years. Developers want the land for luxury hotels. The Vision 2030 authority wants results fast.

options.label: Full heritage-led restoration
options.description: Protect UNESCO status. Expensive, internationally recognized.

options.label: Hybrid development with heritage zones
options.description: Preserve key blocks, allow development elsewhere.

options.label: Prioritize development, preserve facade only
options.description: Maximum economic return. UNESCO compliance minimal.

decisions.title: Vision 2030 Entertainment District
decisions.description: Riyadh has authorized a mixed-gender entertainment district near the Corniche with concerts, cinemas, and international restaurants. The religious establishment has concerns. Economic projections are strong.

options.label: Separate male/female zones only
options.description: Compromise that satisfies no one fully.

decisions.title: Hajj Infrastructure Investment
decisions.description: The airport pilgrim terminal is at 140% capacity annually. An expansion requires 300M and will take 3 years. Alternative: lease additional capacity to private operators immediately.

crises.title: Houthi Ballistic Missile Intercept
crises.description: A Houthi missile was intercepted 15km from Jeddah. Debris fell near a residential area. One injury. Panic in the airport. International media is running 'Jeddah under attack' headlines.

options.label: Emergency public safety alert and shelter order

crises.title: Jeddah Flash Flood
crises.description: A flash flood has submerged the Corniche and Al-Hamra district. Jeddah floods repeatedly due to inadequate drainage — a problem known for decades and unfixed. Deaths are being reported.

options.label: Emergency drainage investment
options.description: Fast-track the fix finally. Very expensive.

options.label: Blame private developers for blocking drainage

crises.title: Hajj Crowd Surge Emergency
crises.description: A crowd surge at a Hajj transit point has injured 200 pilgrims. International Islamic leaders are demanding answers. The Ministry of Hajj is pointing to your city's logistics management.

options.label: Accept responsibility, fund immediate safety overhaul
Redirect blame to federal Hajj authority

crises.title: Desalination Plant Failure
crises.description: Jeddah's primary desalination plant has partially failed. The city depends on desalination for 95% of its water. Rationing begins immediately. Repairs will take three weeks.

options.label: Strict rationing with priority zones
options.description: Hospitals and mosques first. Residential last.

scandals.title: Unauthorized Al-Balad Demolition
scandals.description: A protected heritage building was demolished by a developer with apparent official approval.

scandals.title: Hajj Logistics Contract Fraud
scandals.description: A Hajj transport contract was awarded to a company with no operational experience.

scandals.title: Entertainment License Bribe
scandals.description: An official received payment to fast-track an entertainment license.

positive: Mashallah, a good decision for once.
Jeddah deserves a leader who thinks big.
Progress that honors both faith and future.
Al-Corniche feels alive again. This is the Jeddah we love.
Finally, leadership that sees beyond Tahlia Street and luxury.
My father built this city. Good to see someone protecting what he built.
Even the expats in the compound are saying good things. Rare, wallahi.
neutral: We wait and see as always.
The sea is calm today. That means something.
The papers say one thing, the streets say another. As usual.
Another plan from Riyadh. We'll see what arrives in Jeddah.
Yallah, let's see if the budget even reaches this city.
negative: Another flood. The same promises. Nothing changes.
They build hotels while Al-Balad crumbles.
Jeddah is becoming unaffordable for Jeddawis.
The missiles are real. Our protection is not.
My street has flooded four times since 2009. Where is the drainage?
Vision 2030 is for Riyadh. Jeddah gets the photo ops and the bills.
They replaced a fisherman's home with a five-star resort. Haram.
No water for three days. This is not a city — it's a desert with a logo.
crisis: The pilgrims watch us. So does God.
The Corniche is underwater again. Who is surprised? Nobody.
Every time the rains come, this administration disappears.
Houthi alert and a broken desalination plant in the same week. Yallah.
If the pilgrims see this chaos, the whole nation is shamed.
scandal: Corruption in the city of pilgrims. Shameful.
Money meant for God's guests was stolen.
Al-Balad was protected by UNESCO. Not by us. That says everything.
Hajj contracts go to friends, not builders. The pilgrims suffer for it.
Wallahi, even the Okaz columnist is calling it what it is: theft.
Riyadh will not protect them. This time the embarrassment is too big.
media: Saudi Gazette: 'Jeddah flooding claims lives for third consecutive year — drainage project still unfinished'
Al Arabiya Breaking: Houthi missile intercepted near Jeddah airspace, airport temporarily halted
Okaz: مسؤولون يتهربون من المساءلة بعد كارثة الفيضانات في حي الحمراء
Saudi Gazette: 'Vision 2030 entertainment district opens on Corniche — clerics voice reservations'
Al Arabiya: King Abdulaziz Airport pilgrim terminal at 147% capacity ahead of Hajj season
Okaz: تقرير: 40% من مباني البلد التاريخية في خطر الانهيار رغم وضعها التراثي العالمي
Saudi Gazette: 'Jeddah desalination crisis: Residents queue for tanker water in North Jeddah districts'
Al Arabiya Exclusive: Hajj logistics contract awarded to firm with no prior Hajj experience, sources say
politician: Governor's Office: 'The flooding response was immediate and coordinated. Drainage upgrades are underway.'
Jeddah Municipality: 'Al-Balad restoration is a national priority aligned with Vision 2030 targets.'
Riyadh: Central planning authority reminds Jeddah governorate that entertainment licensing requires federal sign-off.
Hajj Ministry spokesperson: 'Crowd management in Mina is a shared federal responsibility, not a city failure.'
Mayor's statement: 'Water rationing is temporary. The desalination repair team is working around the clock.'
Royal Commission for Jeddah: 'The waterfront megaproject will create 12,000 jobs for Saudi nationals by 2028.'
Ministry of Interior: Security posture around Jeddah remains elevated following Houthi ballistic activity.
Deputy Governor: 'Developers who violate Al-Balad heritage zones will face full legal consequences.' — awaiting outcome
activist: Al-Balad Heritage Society: 'Another protected building lost. UNESCO warned us. The governor was told. Nobody acted.'
Jeddah Flood Victims Network: '2009 flooded us. 2022 flooded us. 2025 flooded us. Same wadi, same neglect.'
Saudi Green Initiative Watch: 'Desalination dependency is a climate time bomb. Diversification is 10 years overdue.'
Hajj Safety Coalition: '200 injured in a crowd surge is not a near-miss — it is a failure of duty of care.'
Al-Balad Heritage Society: 'We submitted the structural risk report in 2021. It was acknowledged and ignored.'
Jeddah Civil Society Forum: 'Vision 2030 must include Jeddawis in planning, not just in the glossy brochures.'
Red Sea Environmental Group: 'Construction runoff from the waterfront project is damaging the reef. No EIA was released.'
Expat Workers Rights Watch: 'Three Filipino workers died on the Corniche construction site. No compensation issued.'
street: Wallahi akhi, every time it rains I move my car to King Fahd Road and sleep in my cousin's flat. Standard.
They built a concert hall on the Corniche. Cool. Still no storm drain on my street in Al-Hamra.
My grandfather's house is in Al-Balad. They say it's heritage. But no one fixes the walls. Only photographs.
The Filipino guys in the compound say they haven't been paid in two months. We know, we just don't say it.
Mashallah the new mall in North Jeddah is beautiful. Inshallah one day the road to get there is also fixed.
Siren went off last night. Houthi missile or a car alarm, nobody knew. That's Jeddah now.
Yallah, they call this Vision 2030. I call it Vision 2030 SAR just to park near the Corniche on a Friday.
My boss is Egyptian, his boss is Pakistani, their boss is Jordanian. The Saudi at the top gets all the credit.

scandal_reactions.minor: The matter has been noted by the relevant religious council.
scandal_reactions.moderate: Community elders have expressed deep concern. A formal response is required.
scandal_reactions.major: Significant backlash from traditional communities. Public support is collapsing.
scandal_reactions.career_ending: This violates the standards expected of a public servant. Step down immediately.

romance_exposure.flavour: This is a profound moral failure. The consequences will be immediate and severe.

city_personality.slang_reaction: Ya sater, aish hal fawda illi aishin fiha!
